ordinary|**further streamlit audit** | rank | finding | confidence | |---|---|---| | 1 | dashboard still crashes from a hidden tab | high | | 2 | some thesis conclusions contradict current artifacts | high | | 3 | shap can explain a different model than the prediction | high | | 4 | every tab performs work on every rerun | high | | 5 | artifact schema protection is incomplete | high | | 6 | polish mode is incomplete | high | **critical findings** 1. the live app now reaches the overview but crashes in thesis conclusions at [streamlit_app.py:1256](c:/users/paula/documents/mgr%20pjatk/streamlit_app.py:1256). the h1 producer outputs `variable` and `value`, but the dashboard requests `intake_type`. because streamlit executes every tab body, this hidden-tab error breaks the entire application. 2. h3 wording conflicts with the target definition and current table at [streamlit_app.py:1259](c:/users/paula/documents/mgr%20pjatk/streamlit_app.py:1259) and [streamlit_app.py:1273](c:/users/paula/documents/mgr%20pjatk/streamlit_app.py:1273). current median days to **any outcome**: - baby: 6.22 days - adult: 6.46 days - senior: 4.20 days therefore, “seniors wait 2–4 times longer” is not supported by this table. it likely confuses adoption-only timing with `regression_target_days`. 3. h1 says intake context is “dramatically stronger” than appearance at [streamlit_app.py:1239](c:/users/paula/documents/mgr%20pjatk/streamlit_app.py:1239), while the displayed shap-family table currently ranks: - breed/appearance: `0.452` - age: `0.422` - intake circumstances: `0.250` - intake condition: `0.182` the conclusion and its adjacent visualization tell different stories. 4. h5 uses causal language such as “covid radically disrupted” and “artificial spike” at [streamlit_app.py:1276](c:/users/paula/documents/mgr%20pjatk/streamlit_app.py:1276). the artifacts establish period differences, not that covid caused them. **model consistency** the prediction function dynamically loads the selected model. current classification selection is histogram gradient boosting. however, local shap at [data.py:594](c:/users/paula/documents/mgr%20pjatk/src/aac_adoption/dashboard/data.py:594) always loads the default catboost model at [data.py:609](c:/users/paula/documents/mgr%20pjatk/src/aac_adoption/dashboard/data.py:609). thus an hgb prediction may be accompanied by a catboost explanation. the sensitivity tab also explicitly claims it uses catboost at [streamlit_app.py:994](c:/users/paula/documents/mgr%20pjatk/streamlit_app.py:994), even though model selection is dynamic. **performance** all 14 tab bodies are declared through one `st.tabs` call at [streamlit_app.py:481](c:/users/paula/documents/mgr%20pjatk/streamlit_app.py:481). every rerun therefore performs hidden-tab work, including: - loading up to 50,000 historical records. - searching similar cases. - loading selected models and predicting an archetype. - potentially computing local shap. caching limits repeated csv reads, but the application still does substantially more work than the visible page requires. **robustness and tests** schema validation is narrower than actual ui requirements. for example, `animal_archetypes` validates only two columns at [data.py:22](c:/users/paula/documents/mgr%20pjatk/src/aac_adoption/dashboard/data.py:22), while its tab requires many additional metrics and profile fields. the application tests patch `aac_adoption.config.project_root`, but `streamlit_app.py` defines its own root and artifact paths at [streamlit_app.py:13](c:/users/paula/documents/mgr%20pjatk/streamlit_app.py:13). consequently, the temporary fixtures are not reliably isolating the app from repository artifacts. tests mainly assert “no exception” and missed both live crashes. **visual and language** the tab-scroll adjustment works: at a 390px viewport there was no page-level horizontal overflow, and tab labels remained single-line. polish mode remains partial: **49 visible strings lack polish translations**, including much of the overview, errors, artifact reader, and thesis conclusions. missing keys silently fall back to english through [streamlit_app.py:340](c:/users/paula/documents/mgr%20pjatk/streamlit_app.py:340). the visual design is good. the remaining problems are primarily runtime contracts, methodological wording, and eager execution rather than aesthetics. no additional files were changed during this analysis. the background pipeline remains active.

ordinary|nie. w obecnie używanych publicznych danych aac **nie mamy dostępu do swobodnych notatek weterynaryjnych ani komentarzy personelu**. - `intakes.csv`: 12 pól, m.in. `intake condition`, rasa, kolor i lokalizacja. - `outcomes.csv`: 12 pól, m.in. `outcome type` i `outcome subtype`. - nowszy zbiór shelterbuddy od **5 maja 2025 r.** również nie udostępnia notatek tekstowych; zawiera tylko pola strukturalne. zbiór outcomes ma dodatkowo `euthanasia_reason`. dlatego zdanie w pracy jest zbyt stanowcze. bezpieczniej napisać: > potencjalnym kierunkiem rozwoju jest pozyskanie, za zgodą aac i po odpowiedniej anonimizacji, nieustrukturyzowanych notatek weterynaryjnych lub behawioralnych. dane te nie są dostępne w publicznych zbiorach wykorzystanych w niniejszej pracy. obecne sygnały zdrowotne i behawioralne pochodzą wyłącznie z kategorii takich jak `intake_condition` oraz `outcome_subtype`, a nie z nlp. źródła: [historyczne intakes aac](https://data.austintexas.gov/d/wter-evkm), [aktualne intakes shelterbuddy](https://data.austintexas.gov/d/pyqf-r2dc), [aktualne outcomes](https://data.austintexas.gov/d/gsvs-ypi7).
